data_source.root: docs/mcm-2015-2025/official_assets/2022/All for One and One (Space) for All!
data_source.source_pdf: docs/mcm-2015-2025/official_assets/2022/All for One and One (Space) for All!

equity_model.definition: Global equity in asteroid mining means broad benefit sharing, fair access to participation, technology diffusion, multilateral voice, risk protection, and peaceful use.

dimensions.benefit_sharing: Mineral value and derived public benefits reach countries irrespective of economic or scientific development.
dimensions.access_to_participation: Emerging space nations, universities, and public agencies can participate in missions, data, and contracts.
dimensions.technology_transfer: Mission knowledge, safety practices, and enabling infrastructure diffuse beyond first movers.
dimensions.governance_voice: Rules are set through transparent multilateral governance, not only by mining states or firms.
dimensions.risk_and_environmental_burden: Launch, reentry, orbital-debris, market, and terrestrial environmental risks are not shifted to low-power countries.
dimensions.peaceful_use_and_conflict_prevention: Resource extraction remains aligned with the Outer Space Treaty and does not militarize access to space resources.

validation_logic: Outer Space Treaty benchmark: higher scores require benefits for all countries irrespective of development level.
International Space Station and satellite access benchmark: multinational access and shared technical infrastructure improve participation equity.
Exclusive concession benchmark: concentrated finance without benefit sharing lowers equity even when technical feasibility is high.

scenarios.description: The UN updates treaty practice through licenses, public reporting, safety rules, and a global benefit fund.
Public agencies, firms, and universities share mission data while firms compete under common benefit obligations.
Several spacefaring states run national programs and form exclusive resource blocs.
A few private firms and sponsor states finance extraction and retain most mineral profits.

recommended_vision.description: The UN updates treaty practice through licenses, public reporting, safety rules, and a global benefit fund.

asteroid_mining_vision.vision_note: The recommended vision balances feasible public-private mining with UN licensing, benefit sharing, data access, and risk bonding.

sensitivity_analysis.method: one-at-a-time deterministic condition sensitivity on the recommended asteroid-mining vision

condition_tests.change: raise global benefit levy from low to high
share mission data and low-cost participation slots
publish ownership, safety, and revenue reports

largest_positive_condition.change: raise global benefit levy from low to high

policies.purpose: Dedicate a transparent share of net mineral value to global public goods and countries without space capability.

policies.policy: open mission data and technology access pool
policies.purpose: Share non-sensitive navigation, prospecting, safety, and materials data with emerging space programs.
Require every extraction mission to disclose sponsor, operator, target body, safety plan, and ownership terms.
Make operators internalize debris, launch, reentry, and terrestrial environmental risks before receiving a license.
Review resource contracts against the Outer Space Treaty principle that outer space benefits all countries.

un_policy_recommendations.package_rule: Adopt the full package: registry and liability rules create enforceability; benefit fund and technology pool create equity gains; treaty review prevents appropriation drift.

one_page_un_memo:
Memo to the UN committee considering an Outer Space Treaty update

The official 2022 ICM-F question asks whether asteroid mining can still benefit all humankind. Our answer is yes, but only if the sector is licensed before exclusive norms harden. The recommended vision is un_licensed_benefit_sharing_regime: public and private mining may proceed, but under UN licensing, benefit sharing, open mission data, and risk bonding. The top sensitivity result is benefit_fund_share; the strongest downside is exclusive_property_claims. The first policy to implement is global benefit-sharing fund, followed by a global benefit-sharing fund and technology access pool. Without these rules, asteroid mining is likely to concentrate profits and voice among first movers; with them, the sector can improve participation, technology diffusion, and peaceful use while remaining technically feasible.

assumption_audit.truthfulness_note: This workflow uses official statement parameters from the COMAP problem and explicit deterministic planning assumptions; it does not use random placeholder data.

assumptions.official_statement_scope: The official problem supplies no numeric attachment; it asks teams to define equity, build a metric, explore asteroid-mining futures, test conditions, and recommend UN policies.
assumptions.score_scale: All dimension scores are deterministic 0-1 planning scores tied to named statement concepts and explicit policy assumptions.
assumptions.treaty_anchor: Outer Space Treaty language is used as the normative validation anchor: space activity should benefit all countries regardless of economic or scientific development.
